page: Amazon Web Services t4g.large is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton2 processor, utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. It features 2 vCPUs mapping to 2 physical cores with single-thread execution. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz and is equipped with 64 KB L1 data cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate that the t4g.large is optimized for bursty, low-to-moderate compute demands rather than sustained high-throughput workloads. In single-core stress-ng tests, the Graviton2 processor achieves an average performance score of 1517.49, placing it in the middle 50% tier. However, multi-core performance scales weakly, scoring 3012.08 in stress-ng multi-core tests, which falls into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show 116.93 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but uncached large-block reads and writes drop to 34.42 GB/sec and 18.39 GB/sec respectively. Synthetic benchmarks like Geekbench and Passmark consistently rank this instance in the bottom 10% tier, reflecting its limited raw computational power and lack of advanced vector instructions like AVX or AMX.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t4g.large offers a highly economical entry point for workloads that do not require continuous CPU utilization. The burstable CPU credit model allows users to pay a lower baseline rate while retaining the ability to burst to full CPU performance when necessary. This resource profile is best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, development and testing environments, code repositories, and small database instances that experience periodic, intermittent traffic spikes rather than sustained heavy processing.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.large is a burstable, ARM64-based virtual machine instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton2 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 1517.49, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into lower performance tiers. Lacking advanced vector extensions like AVX, the t4g.large relies on its burstable CPU credit model to deliver qualitative cost efficiency for intermittent workloads. It is highly suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, development environments, and light database operations that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without continuous high-compute demands.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.large is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring a 2.5 GHz Graviton2 processor, 2 vCPUs, and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for cost-effective, low-utilization workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.large is a burstable ARM64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 2.5 GHz Graviton2 processor for light workloads.
tagline: A cost-effective, burstable ARM64 instance offering balanced memory and baseline performance for low-utilization and intermittent workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton2 ARM64 processor with 2 vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 1 Gbps baseline network
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for qualitative cost efficiency
Average single-core performance suitable for intermittent processing spikes
Best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, and development environments
